title: Rivian R2 Production Launch and Earnings Impact
text: Rivian officially began R2 production at its Normal, Illinois facility on April 22, 2026, just five days after an EF-1 tornado damaged Building 2. The company started production of its R2 electric SUV at its Normal, Illinois plant despite the natural disaster that collapsing a section of the roof. Initial R2 units will go to Rivian employees, with customer deliveries expected by spring's end. The company plans to invite broader customer configurations in June. Analyst projections suggest a gradual ramp: fewer than 400 units in Q2 2026, approximately 7,000 in Q3, and around 15,000 in Q4. The launch model is the Performance AWD at $57,990, significantly higher than the promised $45,000 base price (arriving late 2027). The Performance version offers 656 horsepower, 330 miles of range, and beats the Tesla Model Y on efficiency metrics, though Model Y pricing starts lower at $39,990. Rivian's full-year 2026 guidance targets 62,000-67,000 total deliveries, with the R2 contributing approximately 22,000-23,000 units. The company engineered substantial cost reductions into the platform and targets positive automotive gross margins by the end of 2026.
